trajectory_summary: The call arrived with a prior Ion DQ already on record — Carrie had been told by Ion itself that her home doesn't qualify, and she was fielding repeated follow-up contacts she hadn't requested. Bucky had no awareness of this history and spent the call improvising challenges to a DQ that Ion's own system had already issued. The call ends with the correct outcome (DQ confirmed) but via a reactive, time-burning path that missed the one recoverable revenue move available: a roofing cross-sell bridge. Carrie's civil exit suggests the relationship was not damaged, but no value was created.
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is listed as 'unknown.' The correct outcome classification is disqualified — solar is not viable on this property per Ion's own prior assessment, confirmed again by the manager hold at 03:57. The outcome itself is not in dispute; the gap is that the call should have been flagged as a recycled/prior-DQ lead before Bucky dialed, and the one recoverable path (roofing cross-sell) was not attempted.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: you were right. We're not allowed to do anything because it is too shaky.
primary_coaching_focus.why: The entire call converged on a confirmed solar DQ — the one recoverable revenue move was the roofing cross-sell bridge at the moment of DQ confirmation. Bucky had 54 seconds in a manager hold and returned with only the DQ. Carrie's civil exit confirms she was receptive at close. This is the highest-value coaching gap on the call: Bucky needs to know that a solar DQ is not a dead end — it is a roofing routing trigger per cross_sell_signals.roof_replacement_planned.

key_moments.quote: I was told that solar will not work on my house
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the root-cause moment of the call. Carrie surfaces a prior Ion DQ in her third utterance. The schema home_disqualified rebuttal calls for 'Do you know why it was disqualified?' — a curiosity-opening move that Bucky did not execute. Instead he challenged the DQ via satellite map. The deeper issue is a CRM/lead-routing gap: Ion's own prior consultation result was not visible to Bucky before he dialed, which means he was set up to re-litigate a decision his own company had already made.

key_moments.quote: you guys keep calling me and texting me.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carrie's contact-frequency complaint is a trust signal — she is telling Bucky that Ion has already eroded goodwill through repeated outreach. The schema calls for honest specificity to reduce suspicion. Bucky's response ('I just I'm a little...') is a verbal stumble that neither acknowledges the complaint nor resolves it. He moves immediately to the map challenge, leaving the friction unaddressed.

key_moments.quote: Oh, no. Our house is north south facing. You can't put anything on the front of the house because of the HOA.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carrie is delivering a multi-factor technical home analysis: orientation, HOA restriction, shade coverage, surface area. This is informed-objection territory, not a vulnerability disclosure. Bucky has no structured response — he is being out-argued on technical content by the customer. The setter's scope discipline calls for bridging technical questions to the specialist rather than engaging them directly. Bucky had no bridge available because he had already committed to the map-challenge path.

key_moments.quote: I've been told by another power company. I mean, another solar company the same thing.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the second touch of the prior-DQ pattern — now confirmed by a second independent solar company. The schema home_disqualified rebuttal does not have a canonical response for multi-source DQ confirmation; this is a schema gap. Bucky's response is to immediately escalate to the manager hold rather than acknowledge the second-source confirmation or attempt any pivot. The multi-source confirmation materially changes the recovery calculus — at this point the solar DQ is structurally closed, and the only remaining value move is the roofing cross-sell.
